data.title: As a DSO plugin developer, I can list epics with blocking status via the CLI
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Add `ticket list-epics` subcommand that emits a tab-separated line per open epic with epic_id, title, priority, child_count, and blocker_ids, sorted by priority. Matches sprint-list-epics.sh's output contract byte-for-byte. Supports a `--max-children=<N>` filter.

**Why**: Moves epic listing into the ticket CLI so brainstorm, preplanning, and sprint can consume it through a stable interface rather than invoking sprint-list-epics.sh directly (which bypasses the ticket API).

**Scope**:
- IN: ticket-list-epics.sh (or Python) implementation, dispatcher integration, `--max-children` flag, tab-separated output with blocker_ids field, integration test asserting output equivalence with sprint-list-epics.sh against a fixture, unit tests
- OUT: Refactoring callers of sprint-list-epics.sh (that migration is Epic e1b2-2653's concern)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, `.claude/scripts/dso ticket list-epics` prints a tab-separated line per open epic (epic_id, title, priority, child_count, blocker_ids), sorted by priority
  ← Satisfies: "list-epics (epic listing with blocking status, child counts, priority sorting — replaces sprint-list-epics.sh; preserves tab-separated output format with blocker_ids field)"
- When this story is complete, the output is byte-for-byte equivalent to sprint-list-epics.sh's output on the same fixture (asserted in the integration test)
  ← Satisfies: "preserves tab-separated output format with blocker_ids field"
- When this story is complete, an integration test exercises the subcommand through the CLI dispatcher against a synthetic fixture
  ← Satisfies: "Each new subcommand has an integration test"
- When this story is complete, unit tests are written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow existing ticket-*.sh dispatcher pattern for consistency with sibling subcommands
- [Testing] Output equivalence test should diff the CLI output against sprint-list-epics.sh output on the same fixture

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
